title: Preserve a continuous return path
description: Keep an unbroken reference plane under every high-speed net and add stitching vias at layer transitions.
body: Use when routing any fast signal that changes layers or crosses a plane split. Do not let a signal run over a gap or slot in its reference plane, because the return current is forced into a long loop. Actions: route over solid plane, place stitching vias near layer-change vias, and inspect for plane splits beneath fast nets. Validation signals include a continuous plane under each net on a plane-by-plane review and stitching vias accompanying every layer transition. Risk boundary: a broken return path radiates and couples noise, so treat a signal crossing a plane gap as an EMI defect requiring rework.
